100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 6, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, where discussions focused on disarmament and international peace efforts amid rising tensions in Europe. Meanwhile, in the United States, severe heatwaves continued to grip the Midwest, causing concern over agricultural impacts as farmers struggled with drought conditions. In a significant political development, the British government faced mounting pressure over its handling of the ongoing coal miners' strike, which threatened to disrupt the economy further.
